name: fold_role_columns_019
description: Golden fixture for migration 019_fold_role_columns.sql — the structural frozen-eval Advisor wall. Defines the fold_role values that the wall must handle, the COALESCE filter semantics, and the expected column shape on the fold-partitioned table. Provenance: schema-derived from plan 019 + README §1 hazard table H3. No producer-computed values; all assertions are structural/behavioural.

semantics.null_row_under_correct_filter: included — NULL is not frozen_eval under COALESCE
semantics.null_row_under_buggy_filter: excluded — SQL three-valued logic evaluates NULL != X as NULL/falsy
semantics.frozen_eval_row_under_buggy_filter: excluded — coincidentally correct but only by accident

wall_filter.h3_hazard: A bare fold_role != 'frozen_eval' evaluates NULL to NULL (falsy) and silently hides legitimate train/validation rows from the Advisor. The COALESCE is mandatory.

migration.duplicate_column_swallow: run_migrations swallows 'duplicate column name' — re-applying is safe

advisor_ro_query.description: The only entry the Advisor uses to read from state DB. Any caller SQL touching fold_role must use the COALESCE filter; a bare != is rejected before execution.
advisor_ro_query.bare_predicate_rejection: ValueError raised on bare fold_role != 'frozen_eval' in caller SQL
advisor_ro_query.wall_breach_tripwire: If a frozen_eval row appears in results, writes WALL_BREACH to advisor_observations then raises
